student_description: This student puts in high effort but experiences a 'spinning wheels' effect, failing to master concepts due to a lack of inherent strategic awareness and planfulness. They uniquely require explicit training in self-regulated learning (SRL), modeled study plans, and interventions to reduce assessment anxiety, as they cannot independently translate time invested into academic success.

criteria.rationale: This student invests high hours but lacks strategic direction, requiring explicit examples of how to organize and utilize all available course resources.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan includes providing or co-creating explicit study plans (e.g., a sample student's schedule) that map out exactly when and how to use various course resources.

criteria.rationale: Unlike peers who naturally develop study skills, this student requires sustained, explicit instruction on how to monitor their learning and select appropriate cognitive strategies.
criteria.check: The tutor incorporates ongoing, explicit training on self-regulated learning skills, focusing on how to study, monitor understanding, and choose strategies, rather than solely teaching math content.

criteria.rationale: Traditional lecture formats exacerbate this student's help-avoidance and absenteeism, whereas collaborative environments reduce the perceived threat of the learning space.
criteria.check: The tutor plans for collaborative, interactive learning activities and explicitly avoids relying on traditional, one-way lecture formats.
